leadText: The Master Dojo (Japanese: マスター道場 Master Dojo) is a battle facility on the Isle of Armor in the Galar region. It is owned and operated by Mustard and his wife Honey. The Master Dojo accepts a limited number of students who wish to grow stronger as Pokémon Trainers.

sections.title: Trials
sections.text: Trials [ edit | edit source ] Spoiler warning: this article may contain major plot or ending details. The students who wish to receive the "secret armor" of the Master Dojo must pass Mustard 's three trials: First trial: The students must chase down, battle, and defeat three Galarian Slowpoke , which are at +6 Speed because they are trained by Mustard himself. Additionally, Mustard also asks them to get Klara 's Sw / Avery 's Sh Dojo Uniform back from the Slowpoke. The player succeeds in defeating all three Slowpoke and get the uniform back all on their own, but most students, including the player's rival , managed to catch up to the Slowpoke at least once, passing the first trial. Second trial: The students must find three clusters of Max Mushrooms , mushrooms filled with Dynamax energy that are needed to make a Max Soup , the key to Gigantamaxing . The player and Klara Sw /Avery Sh were the only ones to find three Max Mushroom, passing the second trial. Third trial: The students must participate in a Dynamax battle against each other. Despite Klara Sw /Avery Sh cheating by summoning Toxic Spikes Sw /} Psychic Terrain Sh , the player still defeated them Upon completing the trials, Mustard rewards the player with the "secret armor", the Legendary Pokémon Kubfu . Spoilers end here.

sections.title: Locations
sections.text: Contents 1 Locations 1.1 Battle Court 1.1.1 Restricted Sparring 1.2 Cram-o-matic 1.3 Kitchen 1.3.1 Max Soup 1.3.2 Refrigerator 1.4 Vending machines 1.5 Move Tutor Locations [ edit | edit source ] Battle Court [ edit | edit source ] The Battle Court (Japanese: バトルコート Battle Court ) is a battle area located behind the Master Dojo. Located at a Power Spot, the Battle Court allows Trainers to Dynamax their Pokémon during battle. Restricted Sparring [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Restricted Sparring Restricted Sparring is a form of battle challenge unlocked after defeating Mustard with his strongest team. In it, the player aims for streaks of five or more won battles using a team of Pokémon consisting of a single type . Cram-o-matic [ edit | edit source ] The Cram-o-matic Main article: Cram-o-matic The Cram-o-matic is one of Hyde 's inventions. The player can insert four items into it and get a new one in exchange, following certain recipes. The player can use it at any time after giving Hyde 500 Watts . Kitchen [ edit | edit source ] A bowl of Max Soup Max Soup [ edit | edit source ] After the player has cleared Mustard's second trial, they can talk to a Master Dojo Student in the kitchen in order to have him make them Max Soup (Japanese: ダイスープ Dai Soup ) in exchange for three clusters of Max Mushrooms per serving. The soup can be fed to any Pokémon that has a Gigantamax form (except Melmetal ), which either adds or removes the Gigantamax Factor , depending on whether they already have it or not. For Urshifu , one comb of Max Honey is required in addition to the Max Mushrooms in order to feed it Max Soup. Refrigerator [ edit | edit source ] After donating 100,000 Watts, the refrigerators in the kitchen will be stocked with curry ingredients , from which the player can obtain a maximum of five daily. The ingredient choices are the Fruit Bunch , Moomoo Cheese , Large Leek , and Sausages . Vending machines [ edit | edit source ] Vending machines can be added to the Master Dojo after donating Watts. Right vending machine Fresh Water 30,000 W $ 100 Soda Pop 40,000 W $ 150 Lemonade 50,000 W $ 200 Another vending machine that sells vitamins can be installed in the Master Dojo after more Watts are donated. After donating 200,000 W , the vending machine will only sell Protein and Iron . After donating 300,000 W , the vending machine will also sell Calcium and Zinc . After donating 400,000 W , the vending machine will also sell HP Up and Carbos . The player can choose how many of one vitamin they want to purchase. 1 vitamin for $ 10,000 5 vitamins for $ 40,000 10 vitamins for $ 70,000 25 vitamins for $ 125,000 Move Tutor [ edit | edit source ] After the player has received a Kanto first partner Pokémon from Honey , a Master Dojo Student will offer to teach the player's Pokémon any of the following moves in exchange for five pieces of Armorite Ore per taught move. Move Type Cat. Terrain Pulse Normal Special Burning Jealousy Fire Special Flip Turn Water Physical Grassy Glide Grass Physical Rising Voltage Electric Special Coaching Fighting Status Scorching Sands Ground Special Dual Wingbeat Flying Physical Meteor Beam Rock Special Skitter Smack Bug Physical Triple Axel Ice Physical Corrosive Gas Poison Status Expanding Force Psychic Special Poltergeist Ghost Physical Scale Shot Dragon Physical Lash Out Dark Physical Steel Roller Steel Physical Misty Explosion Fairy Special
